page: OVHcloud d2-4 is an entry-level virtual machine from the Discovery server family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel processor based on the Haswell architecture operating at 2.0 GHz. The server is configured with 2 shared vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 1 thread per core. It includes 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 50 GB local NVMe SSD for storage.

Benchmark results place this instance in the lower performance tiers. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, along with Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores, fall within the bottom 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is limited, with uncached read speeds at 19.19 GB/sec and write speeds at 14.22 GB/sec. Redis SET operations achieve 2.07 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 1.89 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and modest hardware profile, this server represents a budget-friendly option for non-critical workloads. It is suited for lightweight applications such as basic web serving, development environments, small-scale testing, and simple automation tasks that do not require sustained high-performance compute or high network bandwidth.
description: OVHcloud d2-4 is a budget-oriented virtual machine from the Discovery family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 2 shared vCPUs on an Intel x86_64 architecture running at 2.0 GHz, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a 50 GB local NVMe SSD. Performance benchmarks, including Geekbench and stress-ng, consistently place this instance in the bottom 10% of tested servers, reflecting its entry-level hardware profile. It features 1 complimentary public IPv4 address and a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. This server is designed for low-demand workloads, making it a cost-effective option for software development, basic web serving, and lightweight testing environments where high-performance compute and dedicated resources are not required.
